title: RX alloc timeout Kconfig default may cause packet drops on memory-constrained devices
description:
Location: 
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/Kconfig:89-105 (CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_RX_ALLOC_TIMEOUT_MS)
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:750-753 (net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer)

Risk: The default timeout is 10ms with comment explaining tradeoffs. However:

1. The comment says For LoRa low data rate, 10ms is reasonable but LoRa data rate is 300 bps at SF10 so packets arrive slowly. A 10ms wait is actually very short relative to the ~6.8s frame time
2. On memory-constrained devices running multiple protocols, net_pkt buffers may be temporarily exhausted by bursts from other interfaces
3. The comment suggests 0 for K_NO_WAIT but does not explain when this is appropriate

Pattern generating user issue reports: User on STM32L0 with minimal RAM sees sporadic packet drops, does not realize allocation timeout is the cause.

Suggested fix: 
1. Increase default to 50-100ms (still tiny relative to LoRa timing)
2. Add Kconfig help text explaining symptoms of too-short timeout (Failed to allocate RX packet log messages)
3. Document in CLAUDE.md or README that this is a common tuning point for memory-constrained boards
close_reason: Increased default from 10ms to 50ms and added help text mentioning the 'Failed to allocate RX packet' symptom
